title: identity — subscribed events
description: Identity listeners are RARE by design — the module is a substrate, not an event-consumer. The few listeners here connect Identity to the tenancy lifecycle (erasure cascading) and the identity/auth flows (session revocation on credential mutation).

listeners.purpose: Deliberately a no-op. Documented to make explicit that Identity does NOT eagerly react to User provisioning. If we later need reciprocal indexing (e.g. cache invalidation when a User row appears), the listener lives here — today the User → Identity FK is enough.

When the LAST User referencing an Identity is erased AND the caller signals `cascade_identity=true` (rare — typically GDPR-flagged requests), soft-delete the Identity. Idempotent — an Identity with no inbound rows and no explicit cascade flag stays put (it may be reactivated by a fresh registration on the same email during the hold-period).

Synchronous listener that verifies the password_history_hashed rolling window was correctly trimmed to `identity.password.history_size` in the same transaction. Emits a warning metric if the array grew unbounded (defensive — usually the observer already handles this).
Synchronous cache eviction on password change so a request in flight can't reuse a stale cached row for authentication.

Decides whether the lockout warrants a SuspiciousLoginNotification. Signals: (a) request-IP is novel for this Identity in the last 30 days, (b) failed_attempts_count crossed the threshold in under 60 seconds. Both conditions true → dispatch.

When a Tenant is being erased (compliance-driven full-tenant delete), enumerate every Identity whose ONLY inbound User is inside the erased Tenant — those Identities can be soft-deleted alongside. Identities with User rows in other Tenants stay put.

When the KMS provider rotates the key referenced by `identity.mfa_secret.kms.key_id`, schedule a background re-encryption sweep (batched — the `identity:rotate-mfa-secret` command in bulk). Idempotent — secrets already encrypted under the new key are skipped.

notes: The listeners above run on the queue names declared. Retry policy is inherited from the queue's default policy (3 tries, exponential backoff).
Every listener is idempotent by design so a retry after partial success doesn't double-fire downstream effects.
